background: A survey found 17 smokers in a simple random sample (SRS) of size n=57 in a particular community. The task is to compute the 95% confidence interval for the prevalence of smoking in this population.

task.query: What is the adjusted sample size (ñ) and adjusted number of smokers (x̃)?
What is the adjusted proportion (p̃) and its complement (q̃)?
What is the standard error (SE) of the adjusted proportion (p̃)?
What is the 95% confidence interval for the prevalence of smoking in the population?
task.answer: The 95% confidence interval is p̃ ± (1.96 * SE_p̃) = 0.3115 ± 0.1162 = (0.1953 to 0.4277) or approximately (20% to 43%).

meta_info.caption: Confidence interval for population proportion, plus-four  method (Smoking prevalence).
meta_info.section: 16.5 CONFIDENCE INTERVAL FOR A POPULATION PROPORTION
